title: OSIG workbook + brother-loss dream — late-April 2022 fragment
html:
TL;DR

  • Three-line junk-drawer fragment from April 2022: a draft text to "Kaisa" about a combined PDF for the SUS midterm, a flagged "4/40 heavy dream" about losing his brother emilio emotionally (not physically), and the place name Seaside Oregon.
  • Title is "OSIG workbook" but the workbook itself isn't here — note functions as a quick capture during a value-investing-club study session.
  • Dream content is significant: emotional-distance-from-brother dread is a recurring emilio anxiety that also surfaces in 2024–25 family notes.
  • Confirms kaisa (study-group peer) as a 2022 OSU classmate — also referenced in 2022 05 24 c not python cs161 ("let kaisa know" re: Chiquito's graduation).
  • The Seaside tag suggests a coastal-Oregon trip planned or completed around late April 2022.

Key quote

"4/40 heavy dream. About losing my brother not physically but emotionally and other"

Why it matters

Earliest extant entry of the emotional-loss-of-Emilio dream type — a recurring fear that shows up again in the 2024-01-04 dad-state note and several 2025 entries about the Yahir-as-eldest-son burden. This single line is the seed of an entire emotional thread.

The casual coexistence of "SUS midterm PDF" + "heavy dream about losing brother" + "Seaside Oregon" is a clean exhibit of .

Open questions

  • What is "SUS"? (Sustainability course at OSU? — fits with water treatment notes from one week earlier.)
  • Did Yahir actually go to Seaside that spring?
  • Who is kaisa? (Recurs in 2022 05 24 c not python cs161 re: Chiquito's graduation — likely an OSU classmate / family-orbit peer.)

Wikilinks

osig · emilio · kaisa · seaside or · osu · 2022 04 21 water treatment coursework · 2022 05 24 c not python cs161
